description: A designed slogan poster from second-Trump-administration USCIS on a light blue gradient background with a faint, large U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal watermark spanning the frame. At the top, a bold two-line headline in dark navy: 'Break our laws,' (bold upright) with 'lose your privilege.' (italic) beneath it. Centered below the slogan are two stacked, realistic specimen immigration-document images: (top) a sample U.S. 'PERMANENT RESIDENT' green card showing surname 'SPECIMEN', given name 'TEST V', USCIS# 000-000-310, category IR1, with a face photo and Statue of Liberty/flag artwork; (bottom) a sample U.S. immigrant 'VISA' issued by 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON', surname 'TRAVELER', IV Case Number LND2004I6000201, Registration Number 00000473, with a face photo. Both document photos are heavily pixelated/scrambled over the personal-data fields to anonymize them. The visual style is clean, institutional, blue-on-blue with crisp serif typography. The propaganda technique is a blunt deterrence threat: framing lawful immigration status (green card / visa) as a revocable 'privilege' that will be lost if the holder breaks the law, reinforcing the administration's recurring 'privilege not a right' enforcement theme. Target audience: lawful permanent residents and visa holders, and the broader public. Note: this is the same scrambled-document specimen art reused across multiple USCIS posts in this dataset (the OCR mojibake about 'priviledge' and 'SPECIMEN' / 'US CONSULATE GENERAL' confirms reuse).
notable_text: Break our laws, lose your privilege.
